from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# Pre-computed predicate phrases for efficiency
PREDICATE_PHRASING = {
    "country": lambda s, o: f"{s} is from {o}",
    "ingredient": lambda s, o: f"{s} contains {o}",
    "region": lambda s, o: f"{s} originates from {o}",
    "division": lambda s, o: f"{s} belongs to the division {o}",
    "family": lambda s, o: f"{s} is in the family {o}",
    "order": lambda s, o: f"{s} is classified under the order {o}",
    "course": lambda s, o: f"{s} is served as a {o}",
    "dishVariation": lambda s, o: f"{s} has variations such as {o}",
    "mainIngredient": lambda s, o: f"The main ingredients of {s} are {o}",
    "leader": lambda s, o: f"The leader of {s} is {o}",
    "alternativeName": lambda s, o: f"{s} is also known as {o}",
    "creatorOfDish": lambda s, o: f"{s} was created by {o}",
    "creator": lambda s, o: f"{s} was created by {o}",
    "servingTemperature": lambda s, o: f"{s} is served {o}",
    "served": lambda s, o: f"{s} is served {o}",
    "carbohydrate": lambda s, o: f"{s} contains {o} of carbohydrates",
    "fat": lambda s, o: f"{s} contains {o} of fat",
    "protein": lambda s, o: f"{s} contains {o} of protein",
    "year": lambda s, o: f"{s} was introduced in {o}",
    "related": lambda s, o: f"{s} is related to {o}",
    "similarDish": lambda s, o: f"{s} is similar to {o}",
    "genus": lambda s, o: f"{s} belongs to the genus {o}",
    "language": lambda s, o: f"The language of {s} is {o}",
    "isPartOf": lambda s, o: f"{s} is part of {o}",
    "currency": lambda s, o: f"The currency of {s} is {o}",
    "demonym": lambda s, o: f"The demonym for {s} is {o}",
    "capital": lambda s, o: f"The capital of {s} is {o}",
    "ethnicGroup": lambda s, o: f"The ethnic group of {s} is {o}",
    "foundationPlace": lambda s, o: f"{s} was founded in {o}",
    "officialLanguage": lambda s, o: f"The official language of {s} is {o}",
    "leaderTitle": lambda s, o: f"The leader title of {s} is {o}",
    "servingSize": lambda s, o: f"The serving size of {s} is {o}",
}

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    """
    Convert a list of food‑related triples into a single fluent sentence.
    """
    if not triples:
        return ""

    # Group triples by subject for better sentence construction
    subject_triples = {}
    for t in triples:
        if t.subject not in subject_triples:
            subject_triples[t.subject] = []
        subject_triples[t.subject].append(t)

    main_subject = list(subject_triples.keys())[0]

    # Build base sentence for main subject
    main_clauses = []
    for t in subject_triples[main_subject]:
        phrasing_func = PREDICATE_PHRASING.get(t.predicate)
        if phrasing_func:
            main_clauses.append(phrasing_func(t.subject, t.object))

    if not main_clauses:
        return ""

    # Start building the sentence
    sentence_parts = [main_clauses[0]]

    # Add more main subject clauses
    for clause in main_clauses[1:]:
        sentence_parts.append(f", {clause.lower()}")

    # Handle other subjects with relative clauses
    other_subjects = [s for s in subject_triples.keys() if s != main_subject]
    for other_subject in other_subjects:
        other_clauses = []
        for t in subject_triples[other_subject]:
            phrasing_func = PREDICATE_PHRASING.get(t.predicate)
            if phrasing_func:
                other_clauses.append(phrasing_func(t.subject, t.object))

        if other_clauses:
            # Build relative clause for other subject
            if len(other_clauses) == 1:
                sentence_parts.append(f", which is {other_clauses[0].lower()}")
            else:
                sentence_parts.append(f", where the {other_subject.lower()} {', '.join([c.lower() for c in other_clauses])}")

    return "".join(sentence_parts) + "."

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END

@dataclass
class Triple:

    predicate: str
    object: str

# Pre-computed predicate phrases for efficiency
PREDICATE_PHRASING = {
    "country": lambda s, o: f"{s} is from {o}",
    "ingredient": lambda s, o: f"{s} contains {o}",
    "region": lambda s, o: f"{s} originates from {o}",
    "division": lambda s, o: f"{s} belongs to the division {o}",
    "family": lambda s, o: f"{s} is in the family {o}",
    "order": lambda s, o: f"{s} is classified under the order {o}",
    "course": lambda s, o: f"{s} is served as a {o}",
    "dishVariation": lambda s, o: f"{s} has variations such as {o}",
    "mainIngredient": lambda s, o: f"The main ingredients of {s} are {o}",
    "leader": lambda s, o: f"The leader of {s} is {o}",
    "alternativeName": lambda s, o: f"{s} is also known as {o}",
    "creatorOfDish": lambda s, o: f"{s} was created by {o}",
    "creator": lambda s, o: f"{s} was created by {o}",
    "servingTemperature": lambda s, o: f"{s} is served {o}",
    "served": lambda s, o: f"{s} is served {o}",
    "carbohydrate": lambda s, o: f"{s} contains {o} of carbohydrates",
    "fat": lambda s, o: f"{s} contains {o} of fat",
    "protein": lambda s, o: f"{s} contains {o} of protein",
    "year": lambda s, o: f"{s} was introduced in {o}",
    "related": lambda s, o: f"{s} is related to {o}",
    "similarDish": lambda s, o: f"{s} is similar to {o}",
    "genus": lambda s, o: f"{s} belongs to the genus {o}",
    "language": lambda s, o: f"The language of {s} is {o}",
    "isPartOf": lambda s, o: f"{s} is part of {o}",
    "currency": lambda s, o: f"The currency of {s} is {o}",
    "demonym": lambda s, o: f"The demonym for {s} is {o}",
    "capital": lambda s, o: f"The capital of {s} is {o}",
    "ethnicGroup": lambda s, o: f"The ethnic group of {s} is {o}",
    "foundationPlace": lambda s, o: f"{s} was founded in {o}",
    "officialLanguage": lambda s, o: f"The official language of {s} is {o}",
    "leaderTitle": lambda s, o: f"The leader title of {s} is {o}",
    "servingSize": lambda s, o: f"The serving size of {s} is {o}",
}

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    """
    Convert a list of food‑related triples into a single fluent sentence.
    """
    if not triples:
        return ""

    # Build sentence parts efficiently
    parts = []
    main_subject = triples[0].subject

    for t in triples:
        s, p, o = t.subject, t.predicate, t.object
        phrasing_func = PREDICATE_PHRASING.get(p)
        if phrasing_func:
            parts.append((s, phrasing_func(s, o)))
        else:
            parts.append((s, f"{s} {p} {o}"))

    # Build one coherent sentence with relative clauses
    if len(parts) == 1:
        return parts[0][1] + "."

    # Start with main subject
    sentence_parts = [parts[0][1]]

    for s, clause in parts[1:]:
        if s == main_subject:
            # Add as additional info about main subject
            sentence_parts.append(f", {clause.lower()}")
        else:
            # Add as relative clause
            sentence_parts.append(f", where {clause.lower()}")

    return "".join(sentence_parts) + "."

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END
